name: ES8311
description: The `es8311` platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES8311 low power mono audio codec. This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via [Speaker](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) or [Media Player](https://esphome.io/components/media_player/).

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The I²C address of the driver. Defaults to `0x18`.

config_entries.label: I2C ID
config_entries.description: The ID of the [I²C bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) the ES8311 is connected to.

config_entries.label: Bits Per Sample
config_entries.description: The bit depth of the audio samples. One of `16bit`, `24bit`, or `32bit`. Defaults to `16bit`.

config_entries.label: Mic Gain
config_entries.description: The gain applied to the ADC microphones. One of `0DB`, `6DB`, `12DB`, `18DB`, `24DB`, `30DB`, `36DB`, `42DB`. Defaults to `42DB`.

config_entries.label: Use MCLK
config_entries.description: Use the MCLK signal to control the clock. Defaults to `True`.

config_entries.label: Use Microphone
config_entries.description: Configure the codec's ADC to use PDM microphone input instead of analog. Defaults to False.
